nodes.label: Principle I: Multi-tenancy by Default
nodes.rationale: Every feature, data model, and API endpoint MUST be designed with multi-tenancy in mind from day one. Data isolation for the CMS MUST be enforced logically within a shared database using the Payload CMS multi-tenant plugin patterns.

nodes.label: Principle II: AI as a First-Class Citizen
nodes.rationale: The conversational AI agent is not an add-on; it is the primary interface for content schema design and initial content drafting.

nodes.label: Principle III: API-First Content Delivery
nodes.rationale: The CMS is strictly headless. Content delivery MUST be exposed via robust, read-only JSON APIs.

nodes.label: Principle IV: Test-First TDD
nodes.rationale: Test-Driven Development (TDD) is mandatory. Tests must be written before implementation, focusing heavily on tenant isolation (logical boundaries), AI orchestration, and API response performance.

nodes.label: Principle V: Developer Experience DX
nodes.rationale: The platform must provide a frictionless experience for developers building on top of it. This includes managed front-end starter templates, comprehensive documentation, and predictable API structures.

nodes.label: Principle VI: Strict Domain-Driven Design
nodes.rationale: The codebase MUST adhere strictly to Domain-Driven Design principles, especially for backend microservices outside the core CMS. Business logic must be encapsulated within distinct Bounded Contexts.

nodes.label: Principle VII: Hybrid Architecture
nodes.rationale: The core Content Management System MUST be built as a modular monolith using Payload CMS. However, heavy backend processing, specifically the AI Agents and orchestration logic, MUST be implemented as independent microservices adhering to strict DDD principles.
